mission.title: Install and Configure Kubevela on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeVela is a modern application delivery platform that simplifies deploying and operating applications across hybrid and multi-cloud environments. Installing KubeVela allows you to manage your applications more efficiently and with greater reliability.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KubeVela Helm repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kubegems https://charts.kubegems.io/kubegems
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KubeVela using Helm
steps.description:
Install KubeVela with the following command, specifying the namespace and version:
```bash
helm install kubevela kubegems/kubevela --namespace kubevela --create-namespace --version 1.3.5
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the KubeVela pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To set resource limits for the KubeVela controller, edit the values.yaml file or use the following command:
```bash
kubectl patch deployment kubevela-controller -n kubevela --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KubeVela release with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall kubevela --namespace kubevela
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were installed:
```bash
kubectl delete crd applications.core.oam.dev
kubectl delete crd definitions.core.oam.dev
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Finally, delete the KubeVela namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kubevela
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of KubeVela:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevela -o yaml > kubevela-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Helm repository:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeVela
upgrade.description:
Upgrade KubeVela to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade kubevela kubegems/kubevela --namespace kubevela --version 1.3.5
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the KubeVela pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see pods in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for more information:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubevela
``` 
Common fixes include checking configuration files and ensuring all required resources are available.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'release already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating that the release already exists, you can either uninstall the existing release or upgrade it:
```bash
helm uninstall kubevela --namespace kubevela
```

troubleshooting.title: KubeVela controller not responding
troubleshooting.description:
If the KubeVela controller is not responding, check the deployment status:
```bash
kubectl get deployment kubevela-controller -n kubevela
``` 
If it's not available, consider redeploying the controller.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeVela will show all pods in the kubevela namespace running without errors. You should also be able to access the KubeVela dashboard and manage your applications.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubegems https://charts.kubegems.io/kubegems
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
